settings.heading: Candy with a Chicago story.
settings.subheading: We carry forward the Cupid Candies tradition — hand-poured, hand-boxed, and shipped fresh from 75th Street the way Chicago has loved them for generations.

settings.image_alt: Stephanie Hart at the historic Cupid Candies factory
settings.heading: From Cupid Candies to Brown Sugar.
settings.body:
In 1936, a Greek immigrant named Paul Stefanos opened a little candy shop on Chicago's South Side and started turning out fudge, toffee, and popcorn. Four years later he invented the three-layer meltaway that became a Marshall Field's holiday standard. For 84 years, the Stefanos family hand-poured chocolate at the Western Avenue factory — quietly making the Chicago candy our parents grew up on.

In 2020, I took over that factory. The same recipes. The same chocolate makers — many of them with thirty years on the line. We didn't reinvent anything. We just kept the lights on, the kettles hot, and the tradition going. Now Cupid lives under the Brown Sugar roof on 75th Street. Same recipes. Same hands-on care. Made in Chicago, the way it's always been made.

Stocked corporate gift programs, custom-branded boxes, and large-volume orders welcome — we love helping companies send something a little more personal than the usual.

settings.caption: The 1940 original — three layers, one bite
Cooked the long way, the way Paul did it

settings.heading: The candies you remember.
settings.subheading: Traditional flavors crafted the old way — fifty years of small-batch, hand-poured artistry on Chicago's South Side.

settings.heading: A few good places to begin.
settings.subheading: Not the full shelf — just the boxes our regulars come back for. A good first taste of the kitchen.

settings.heading: Larger order? Let's talk.
settings.subheading: Custom-branded boxes, recurring corporate gift programs, retail wholesale — tell us what you need and we'll come back with options.

settings.question: Do you offer in-store pickup for candy orders?
settings.answer: No — candy ordering on this site is shipping only. Every online order goes out from our 75th Street kitchen via shipping. If you're in Chicago and want to walk in, the 75th Street store has retail candy on the shelf — stop by, we'd love to see you. But there's no pickup pathway for the orders placed here.

settings.question: How long does the candy stay fresh?
settings.answer: Our chocolates are at their best within 4–6 weeks of the day they're poured. Caramels and brittles will hold a little longer if kept cool and sealed. We don't use preservatives, so the fresher the better — store in a cool, dry spot away from direct sun, and don't refrigerate the chocolates (the cold pulls moisture and dulls the finish).

settings.question: Do you ship in the summer?
settings.answer: Yes — but carefully. From late May through September, all chocolate orders ship with insulated liners and ice packs, and we recommend selecting expedited shipping so the box doesn't sit on a porch in the heat. If your delivery zone is in a heat wave the week you order, we'll reach out before we ship. We'd rather hold the order a day than send you a melted box.

settings.question: Are any of your candies gluten-free, dairy-free, or nut-free?
settings.answer: Many of our caramels, brittles, and dark chocolate pieces are naturally gluten-free, and we have a few dairy-free options. Allergens vary by product — every PDP lists what's in the box. We pour candy on shared equipment, though, so we can't promise a fully nut-free or dairy-free environment. If you have a serious allergy, give us a call at 773-570-7676 before ordering and we'll walk you through what's safe.

settings.question: Can you do custom-branded boxes or corporate gift programs?
settings.answer: Yes — and we love this work. Custom labels, ribbons, printed cards, branded boxes, recurring quarterly gift drops, holiday client sends — all on the table. The wholesale & corporate inquiry form just above this section is the easiest way to start the conversation; tell us roughly what you have in mind and we'll come back with options and pricing.

settings.question: Can I order in bulk for an event?
settings.answer: Absolutely. We do candy spreads for weddings, holiday parties, corporate gifts, and dessert tables all the time. Tell us your guest count and your date in the inquiry form above and we'll put together a quote — minimum lead time is usually two weeks, longer for the holidays.

settings.question: Where does your chocolate come from?
settings.answer: Our chocolate is poured in Chicago at the Cupid Candies factory on Western Avenue — the same factory that's been operating since 1956. We rely on the traditions of Chicago-style American chocolate: soft, perfectly set, silky smooth. Not the shiny, hard, perfectly tempered European style — ours is the chocolate that takes you back to a simpler time.

settings.heading: Sweeten the next occasion.
settings.subheading: Ship a tin nationwide. Stock a corporate gift program. Pick up a box on 75th.
settings.microcopy: Hand-poured in Chicago. Boxed the same week it ships.
